hero.title: 9 Days, Royal Rajasthan.
hero.titleAccent: Forts, Palaces, Lakes and the Thar Desert.
hero.intro: Rajasthan in all its glory. The Pink City of Jaipur, the blue lanes of Jodhpur, the lake palaces of Udaipur, and a camel safari into the golden Thar Desert at Jaisalmer. Nine days of forts, palaces and royal romance.
hero.heroImageAlt: Rajasthan forts palaces and Thar desert camel safari

overview.heading: 9 Days Royal Rajasthan Desert Safari: The Full Picture
body: Rajasthan is the land of kings, and this 9 day tour captures its full romance. From Jaipur's Pink City to the blue houses and mighty Mehrangarh Fort of Jodhpur, the serene lake palaces of Udaipur, and the golden sandstone city of Jaisalmer with its desert camel safari, it is a journey through India's most regal state.

Arranged privately from Malaysia, the trip links the great Rajput cities with your own vehicle and driver guide, and includes a night in a desert camp under the Thar's star-filled sky. Forts, palaces, lakes, bazaars and the desert combine into one of the most colourful and rewarding tours in India.

highlights.heading: Why Travellers from Malaysia Love This Trip

items.title: Forts & Palaces
items.body: Jaipur's Amber Fort, Jodhpur's towering Mehrangarh, and Udaipur's romantic City Palace and lake palaces, the grandest of Rajput architecture.

items.title: Thar Desert Camel Safari
items.body: A camel safari into the golden dunes near Jaisalmer, with a night in a desert camp under the stars, music and dance, the romance of the Thar.

items.title: Blue City & Lake City
items.body: The blue-painted old town of Jodhpur and the serene lakes and palaces of Udaipur, two of the most beautiful cities in India.
items.imageAlt: Jodhpur blue city and Udaipur lakes

whyBook.heading: Royal Rajasthan, the Way It Should Be
whyBook.body: Rajasthan's cities are far apart and rich in heritage, and the desert safari needs the right camp. We route the journey, choose characterful stays and handle the safari. Dhesu has run Rajasthan tours for over 35 years.

points.title: MATTA & IATA Registered
points.body: Fully licensed Malaysian travel agency. We guide you through India e-Visa documentation with every booking.

points.title: Private Tours, Daily Departures
points.body: No minimum group to wait for. Your trip runs on your dates, your pace, your own vehicle and guide.

points.title: A Real Team to Call
points.body: Reachable throughout your trip. Flight delayed, your guide waits. Plans change, we adapt on the ground.

points.title: Group & Custom Pricing
points.body: Travelling as a family or group lowers the per-person cost. Ask us for a tailored quote.

sections.heading: Royal Rajasthan Desert Safari Tour from Malaysia
sections.body:
This 9 day tour captures the full romance of Rajasthan: the Pink City of Jaipur, the Blue City of Jodhpur, the golden desert city of Jaisalmer with its camel safari, and the lake city of Udaipur.

A private vehicle and driver guide link the great Rajput cities, with characterful heritage stays and a night in a desert camp under the Thar's stars.

It is one of the most colourful and rewarding tours in India, ideal for lovers of history, architecture and romance.

sections.heading: The Cities of Rajasthan
sections.body:
Each Rajasthan city has its own character and colour. Jaipur is the Pink City of forts and bazaars; Jodhpur the Blue City beneath the mighty Mehrangarh Fort; Jaisalmer the golden sandstone city at the edge of the Thar Desert; and Udaipur the white lake city, the most romantic of all.

Together with a desert camel safari, they make a journey through India's most regal and photogenic state.

items.question: Do Malaysians need a visa for India?
items.answer: Yes. Malaysian passport holders apply for an India e-Visa online before departure. The tourist e-Visa covers tourism and short visits and is valid for 30 days from entry. The application is on the official India e-Visa portal, and Dhesu walks you through it when you book. Visa fees are not included in the package price.

items.question: Which airport does this trip use?
items.answer: This is a land-only package: hotels, transfers, tours and guide are included, but international flights are not. Most Malaysian travellers fly from Kuala Lumpur International Airport (KLIA) to Jaipur and Udaipur Airports (JAI / UDR). We can recommend suitable flights and timings on request.

items.question: What is the best time for a Rajasthan tour?
items.answer: October to March is ideal, with pleasant days and cool nights, perfect for forts and the desert. April to June is extremely hot, often above 40 degrees. The desert is especially beautiful in the cooler winter months.

items.question: Is the desert camp comfortable?
items.answer: Yes. Desert camps near Jaisalmer range from comfortable tents to luxury options, with attached bathrooms, dining, and evening entertainment of Rajasthani music and dance. The night under the Thar's stars is a highlight for most travellers.

items.question: How much driving is involved?
items.answer: Rajasthan's cities are spread out, so there are some long, scenic drives, notably Jaisalmer to Udaipur. The nine-day pace keeps each day manageable, and the private vehicle makes the longer legs comfortable with stops along the way.
